text.title: GKE Cluster Architecture Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the gcp-gke-architecture methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: Use GKE Autopilot for all new clusters unless the workload needs GPU, privileged containers, custom OS-level node configuration or Windows nodes.
rule.rationale: Autopilot covers 80%+ of use cases with materially less operational toil than Standard.

rule.statement: Provision regional clusters (3 zones) for production and zonal clusters for development / preview.
rule.rationale: Regional control plane survives a single-zone outage; zonal is cheaper for ephemeral environments where downtime is acceptable.

rule.statement: Create at minimum a general on-demand pool, a Spot pool (min=0, max=N) with taint, and — when needed — a tainted GPU pool.
rule.rationale: Workload-class-aware pools let autoscaler scale Spot to zero without disturbing always-on services.

rule.statement: Enable Workload Identity on every cluster and bind Kubernetes ServiceAccounts to GCP service accounts via annotations; never mount key files.
rule.rationale: Mounted keys break the same exposure model that Workload Identity exists to eliminate.

rule.statement: Provision clusters as private clusters with authorised networks restricted to the corporate IP range and Cloud NAT for egress.
rule.rationale: Public control planes are the recurring entry point for GKE incident reports — private-cluster is the default.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
